market_context: Markets are under severe pressure from the escalating U.S.-Iran conflict, with futures down sharply (S&P -1.4%, Nasdaq -2.17%, Dow -1.45%) and oil spiking to $75. The VIX has jumped over 10% as investors flee to safe havens like gold ($5,270). Major earnings from Target, CrowdStrike, and others today provide potential volatility.

predictions.reasoning: QQQ futures are down 2.17% premarket with tech under pressure from geopolitical tensions and AI fears. Current overnight price of 604.22 shows weakness, and historical patterns during geopolitical crises suggest continued selling pressure.

S&P 500 futures down 1.66% on Iran conflict escalation with oil prices spiking. March 2nd close at 686.71 shows vulnerability as markets typically decline 2-4% during early stages of Middle East conflicts before finding support.

CrowdStrike earnings after hours with EPS estimate of $1.10 vs $1.03 prior year. Despite being down 3% premarket, cybersecurity demand increases during geopolitical tensions and recent AI competition fears may be overdone.

Target reports earnings this morning with EPS estimate of $2.16. Recent guidance was optimistic and defensive retail names have shown resilience during market stress. Consumer staples typically outperform during geopolitical uncertainty.

Oil prices surged 5% to $75/barrel on Iran conflict with potential Strait of Hormuz closure threats. Exxon gained 1.1% yesterday and energy stocks historically outperform significantly during Middle East supply disruptions.
